## Before submitting

Please consider the following aspects:

- search existing issues to avoid duplicates
- check vignette and package documentation
- check the [DHARMa GitHub wiki](https://github.com/florianhartig/DHARMa/wiki)


## Contents of the issue

The most suitable content of the issue depends on whether the issue is about a feature request, problem, error or question in your practical work with DHARMa. 
In most cases, it is helpful to follow this structure:

1. Motivation of the study.
2. Your model and DHARMa code as you execute it (ideally as a reproducible example, see [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/5963269/how-to-make-a-great-r-reproducible-example))
3. DHARMa results / error messages
4. Your question
